title: C# 7 Cheat Sheet
description: Key features and syntax of C# 7

commands.command: if (obj is string s) { Console.WriteLine($"String length: {s.Length}"); }
switch (shape) { case Circle c: Console.WriteLine($"Circle with radius {c.Radius}"); break; case Rectangle r: Console.WriteLine($"Rectangle with width {r.Width}"); break; }
switch (shape) { case Circle c when c.Radius > 10: Console.WriteLine("Large circle"); break; }
commands.description: Using 'when' clause in switch pattern matching
commands.scenario: Using 'when' clause in switch pattern matching

commands.command: void OuterMethod() { int LocalFunction(int x) => x * x; Console.WriteLine(LocalFunction(5)); }
commands.description: Define a local function within a method
commands.scenario: Define a local function within a method

commands.command: int Factorial(int n) { return n <= 1 ? 1 : n * Factorial(n - 1); }
void OuterMethod() { int y = 5; int LocalFunction(int x) => x + y; Console.WriteLine(LocalFunction(3)); }
bool IsValid(string input, out int number) { return int.TryParse(input, out number); }
commands.description: Use out variables in method calls
commands.scenario: Use out variables in method calls

commands.command: bool ParseCoordinates(string input, out int x, out int y) { var parts = input.Split(','); return int.TryParse(parts[0], out x) && int.TryParse(parts[1], out y); }
commands.description: Multiple out variables in a single method
commands.scenario: Multiple out variables in a single method

commands.command: ref int Find(int[] numbers, int target) { for (int i = 0; i < numbers.Length; i++) { if (numbers[i] == target) return ref numbers[i]; } throw new IndexOutOfRangeException(); }
commands.description: Return a reference to a local variable
commands.scenario: Return a reference to a local variable

commands.command: ref int number = ref Find(numbers, 5); number = 10;
commands.description: Modify a value using ref return
commands.scenario: Modify a value using ref return

commands.command: ref int refLocal = ref numbers[0]; refLocal = 42;
commands.description: Use ref locals to hold references
commands.scenario: Use ref locals to hold references
